stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In a 2019 study, researchers in Oregon investigated how often urban red foxes returned to known foraging sites during the autumn season.
  • The researchers established 24 monitored bait stations across the study area and fitted 92 red foxes with GPS collars.
  • The collars logged each visit a fox made to a bait station.
  • 41 foxes (44.6%) returned to only one station.
  • 11 foxes (12.0%) returned to 8 or more stations.
  • Foxes with smaller home ranges tended to return to multiple stations.
stem: The student wants to present the methods used in the 2019 study. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: By logging each fox’s visits to bait stations, the researchers discovered that only a relatively small percentage (12.0%) of the foxes returned to 8 or more stations.
The researchers fitted 92 urban red foxes with GPS collars and logged each fox’s visits to 24 monitored bait stations across the study area.
After tracking how many bait stations the 92 red foxes returned to, the researchers found that foxes with smaller home ranges tended to return to more stations.
The researchers inspected each bait station for signs that it had been visited by urban red foxes.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence presents the methods used in the study, noting that researchers fitted 92 urban red foxes with GPS collars and logged each fox’s visits to 24 monitored bait stations.

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence mentions an aspect of the study’s design (that the researchers logged each fox’s visits), it primarily focuses on a finding of the study rather than the methods the researchers used. Choice C is incorrect. While the sentence mentions an aspect of the study’s design (that the researchers tracked the foxes’ visits), it primarily focuses on a finding of the study rather than the methods. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence misrepresents information from the notes. The researchers used GPS collars to log the foxes’ visits; they didn’t inspect each station for evidence of visits.
